purpose: Check whether the remaining short level-intro rectangle on the promoted tnd8040 candidate is caused by the cameraBufferToggle resolution-framebuffer redirect or camera viewport width/height constants.

base_candidate.name: tnd8040
base_candidate.summary: Promoted tnd58 derivative with low framebuffer base/clear moved from 0x80300000 to 0x80400000.

stage_label_correction.p13end: The End / end credits stage / City of Hamburg
stage_label_correction.note: Do not describe the p13end white-rectangle or black-screen behavior as Party. It is the end-credits level signal.

candidates.name: t8040nr

hardware_results.result: Still boots through the Party title/intro into live rendered scenes. The short top intro/title rectangle remains essentially unchanged from tnd8040.
Still boots into City of Hamburg/end-credits rendering. The short top intro/title rectangle remains essentially unchanged from tnd8040.

candidates.interpretation: Noresfb is an informative miss on the tnd8040 base. The remaining intro-rectangle issue is probably not caused by the viSetFrameBuf2(resolution) redirect alone.

candidates.name: t8040camstk
candidates.patch: Restore only cameraBufferToggle viewport width/heights at 0xBB7A4, 0xBB89C, 0xBB8B8, and 0xBB8C0 to stock TND words.

hardware_results.result: Still boots into live Party rendering. The Party title/intro card remains in the same short top strip class, so the stock camera constants do not explain the rectangle by themselves.

candidates.interpretation: The short level-intro/title strip is not solved by restoring camera viewport width/heights. Deprioritize cameraBufferToggle constants as the next main branch.

current_conclusion: Keep tnd8040 as the promoted playability candidate unless manual testing finds a regression.
Treat noresfb and stock-camera follow-ups as misses for the remaining short intro rectangle.
Next technical branch should inspect the title/intro overlay, front/cutscene blitter, or display-copy path rather than rerunning broad camera-viewport reverts.
